rules.name: dag-title-case-prose-edit
rules.evidence: Write **Dag** (title case) in all prose. Keep the all-caps or lowercase spelling only when reproducing a literal code token — never rewrite these

rules.name: dag-title-case-prose-write
rules.evidence: Write **Dag** (title case) in all prose. Keep the all-caps or lowercase spelling only when reproducing a literal code token — never rewrite these

rules.name: no-spell-out-directed-acyclic-graph-edit
rules.evidence: Don't spell out **Directed Acyclic Graph** except for historical context.

rules.name: no-spell-out-directed-acyclic-graph-write
rules.evidence: Don't spell out **Directed Acyclic Graph** except for historical context.

rules.name: no-direct-pytest-python-airflow-on-host
rules.evidence: Never run pytest, python, or airflow commands directly on the host — always use `breeze`.

rules.name: place-temp-scripts-in-dev
rules.evidence: Place temporary scripts in `dev/` (mounted as `/opt/airflow/dev/` inside Breeze).

rules.name: ruff-format-check-after-edit
rules.evidence: Always format and check Python files with ruff immediately after writing or editing them ... Do this for every Python file you create or modify, before moving on to the next step.

rules.name: ruff-format-check-after-write
rules.evidence: Always format and check Python files with ruff immediately after writing or editing them ... Do this for every Python file you create or modify, before moving on to the next step.

rules.name: no-assert-in-production-code-edit
rules.evidence: No `assert` in production code.

rules.name: no-assert-in-production-code-write
rules.evidence: No `assert` in production code.

rules.name: use-monotonic-not-time-time-edit
rules.evidence: `time.monotonic()` for durations, not `time.time()`.

rules.name: use-monotonic-not-time-time-write
rules.evidence: `time.monotonic()` for durations, not `time.time()`.

rules.name: no-session-commit-airflow-core-edit
rules.evidence: In `airflow-core`, functions with a `session` parameter must not call `session.commit()`.

rules.name: no-session-commit-airflow-core-write
rules.evidence: In `airflow-core`, functions with a `session` parameter must not call `session.commit()`.

rules.name: session-keyword-only-param-edit
rules.evidence: Use keyword-only `session` parameters.

rules.name: session-keyword-only-param-write
rules.evidence: Use keyword-only `session` parameters.

rules.name: imports-at-top-of-file-edit
rules.evidence: Imports at top of file. Valid exceptions: circular imports, lazy loading for worker isolation, `TYPE_CHECKING` blocks.

rules.name: imports-at-top-of-file-write
rules.evidence: Imports at top of file. Valid exceptions: circular imports, lazy loading for worker isolation, `TYPE_CHECKING` blocks.

rules.name: guard-heavy-type-only-imports-edit
rules.evidence: Guard heavy type-only imports (e.g., `kubernetes.client`) with `TYPE_CHECKING` in multi-process code paths.

rules.name: guard-heavy-type-only-imports-write
rules.evidence: Guard heavy type-only imports (e.g., `kubernetes.client`) with `TYPE_CHECKING` in multi-process code paths.

rules.name: no-new-raise-airflow-exception-edit
rules.evidence: Never add new direct `raise AirflowException(...)` usages — the community is actively reducing them, not adding more, and the `check-no-new-airflow-exceptions` prek hook enforces this.

rules.name: no-new-raise-airflow-exception-write
rules.evidence: Never add new direct `raise AirflowException(...)` usages — the community is actively reducing them, not adding more, and the `check-no-new-airflow-exceptions` prek hook enforces this.

rules.name: translate-domain-exceptions-to-httpexception-edit
rules.evidence: In `airflow-core/src/airflow/core_api/` route handlers, catch errors raised by domain code ... and re-raise as `HTTPException` with the right status ... Otherwise they propagate as `500 Internal Server Error`, leaking internals and misleading clients.

rules.name: translate-domain-exceptions-to-httpexception-write
rules.evidence: In `airflow-core/src/airflow/core_api/` route handlers, catch errors raised by domain code ... and re-raise as `HTTPException` with the right status.

rules.name: batch-bulk-delete-update-scheduler-edit
rules.evidence: Bulk `DELETE`/`UPDATE` in the scheduler loop or any synchronous interval task ... must be batched with `LIMIT` and committed between batches — never issue a single unbounded bulk write against a user-driven table.

rules.name: batch-bulk-delete-update-scheduler-write
rules.evidence: Bulk `DELETE`/`UPDATE` in the scheduler loop or any synchronous interval task ... must be batched with `LIMIT` and committed between batches — never issue a single unbounded bulk write against a user-driven table.

rules.name: name-functions-action-verbs-edit
rules.evidence: Name functions and methods with action verbs: `get_`, `extract_`, `find_`, `compute_`, `build_`, etc. Avoid noun-only names like `_serialize_keys` or `_base_names`.

rules.name: name-functions-action-verbs-write
rules.evidence: Name functions and methods with action verbs: `get_`, `extract_`, `find_`, `compute_`, `build_`, etc. Avoid noun-only names like `_serialize_keys` or `_base_names`.

rules.name: apache-license-header-new-files
rules.evidence: Apache License header on all new files (prek enforces this).

rules.name: use-pytest-not-unittest-testcase-edit
rules.evidence: Use pytest patterns, not `unittest.TestCase`.

rules.name: use-pytest-not-unittest-testcase-write
rules.evidence: Use pytest patterns, not `unittest.TestCase`.

rules.name: use-spec-autospec-when-mocking-edit
rules.evidence: Use `spec`/`autospec` when mocking.

rules.name: use-spec-autospec-when-mocking-write
rules.evidence: Use `spec`/`autospec` when mocking.

rules.name: prefer-mock-patch-decorator-edit
rules.evidence: Prefer `@mock.patch` decorators over `with mock.patch(...)` context managers for patching.

rules.name: prefer-mock-patch-decorator-write
rules.evidence: Prefer `@mock.patch` decorators over `with mock.patch(...)` context managers for patching.

rules.name: no-datetime-now-in-tests-edit
rules.evidence: Use `time_machine` for time-dependent tests. Do not use `datetime.now()`

rules.name: no-datetime-now-in-tests-write
rules.evidence: Use `time_machine` for time-dependent tests. Do not use `datetime.now()`

rules.name: no-assert-raw-log-text-edit
rules.evidence: Do not assert on raw log text (`caplog.text` for example), these are legacy string matching APIs planned for removal.

rules.name: no-assert-raw-log-text-write
rules.evidence: Do not assert on raw log text (`caplog.text` for example), these are legacy string matching APIs planned for removal.

rules.name: commit-message-user-impact
rules.evidence: Write commit messages focused on user impact, not implementation details.

rules.name: commit-no-conventional-commits-prefix
rules.evidence: Use the imperative mood and a plain message — do **not** use Conventional Commits prefixes (`fix:`, `feat:`, `chore:`, `docs:`, `refactor:`, …). apache/airflow does not follow that convention.

rules.name: commit-body-why-not-what
rules.evidence: The commit message **body** should describe **why** the change is made — the motivation and context — and **never what** the change is.

rules.name: run-prek-install-before-commit
rules.evidence: **Always run `prek install` before committing any code.** It installs the `commit-msg` hook ... so the Conventional Commits guard runs locally.

rules.name: no-co-authored-by-self
rules.evidence: NEVER add Co-Authored-By with yourself as co-author of the commit. Agents cannot be authors, humans can be, Agents are assistants.

rules.name: no-newsfragments-providers-airflow-ctl
rules.evidence: Never add newsfragments for `providers/` or `airflow-ctl/` — those distributions are released from `main` and their release managers regenerate the changelog from `git log`.

rules.name: newsfragment-certainty-check
rules.evidence: Golden rule: never create a newsfragment unless you are certain the change is user-facing ... Default to omitting it; a maintainer will ask for a newsfragment during review if the change warrants one.

rules.name: verify-git-remote-naming-before-remote-command
rules.evidence: Before running any remote-based command, run `git remote -v` and verify the names match this convention.

rules.name: never-push-to-upstream-or-main
rules.evidence: Always push branches to `origin`. Never push directly to `upstream` (and never push directly to `main` on either remote).

rules.name: check-existing-pr-before-creating
rules.evidence: Before working on an issue, check for open PRs already addressing it ... Do not blindly open another near-identical PR for an issue that already has one (or several) — that just adds reviewer noise.

rules.name: pre-push-self-review-checklist
rules.evidence: Before pushing, perform a self-review of your changes ... 1. Review the full diff ... 2. Read `.github/instructions/code-review.instructions.md` ... 4. Run regular (fast) static checks ... 8. Check for security issues.

rules.name: pre-push-rebase-onto-target
rules.evidence: Before pushing, always rebase your branch onto the latest target branch (usually `main`) to avoid merge conflicts and ensure CI runs against up-to-date code.

rules.name: pr-title-no-conventional-commits
rules.evidence: Review the PR title — keep it short (under 70 chars), in the imperative mood, and focused on user impact. Do not use Conventional Commits prefixes (`fix:`, `feat:`, `chore:`, …).

rules.name: no-issue-before-pr-golden-rule
rules.evidence: If you already know how to fix the problem and you (or the user) are going to open the PR shortly, **do not file a GitHub issue first**. Go straight to the PR.

rules.name: attribution-footer-for-agent-github-messages
rules.evidence: Anything an agent drafts that ends up posted to GitHub on the user's account — PR / issue comments, PR-level reviews, line-level review comments, discussion replies — must end with an attribution footer.

rules.name: no-tagging-individuals-github-messages
rules.evidence: AI agents MUST NOT mention or tag individual contributors, committers, PMC members, or maintainers using GitHub usernames (e.g. `@user`) unless explicitly instructed by a human reviewer.

rules.name: never-edit-apache-magpie-snapshot
rules.evidence: Adopter-specific modifications to framework-skill workflows live in `.apache-magpie-overrides/` — never edit the snapshot directly.

rules.name: ask-first-destructive-migration-changes-edit
rules.evidence: **Ask first**: ... Destructive data or migration changes.

rules.name: ask-first-destructive-migration-changes-write
rules.evidence: **Ask first**: ... Destructive data or migration changes.

rules.name: never-commit-secrets-credentials-tokens-edit
rules.evidence: **Never**: Commit secrets, credentials, or tokens.

rules.name: never-commit-secrets-credentials-tokens-write
rules.evidence: **Never**: Commit secrets, credentials, or tokens.

rules.name: never-hand-edit-generated-uv-lock
rules.evidence: **Never**: ... Edit generated files by hand when a generation workflow exists. [The `uv.lock` file is generated by `uv lock`, `uv sync`...]

rules.name: never-use-destructive-git-operations
rules.evidence: **Never**: ... Use destructive git operations unless explicitly requested.

skill_candidates.summary: One-time developer environment setup: install prek, enable the commit-msg hook, and install the per-machine breeze shim.
skill_candidates.evidence: Install prek: `uv tool install prek` - Enable commit hooks: `prek install` - Install breeze shim (one-time, per machine): `scripts/tools/setup_breeze` — installs `~/.local/bin/breeze` that runs breeze via `uvx` from the current git worktree's `dev/breeze`.

skill_candidates.summary: Open and cross-link a GitHub tracking issue when a PR ships a workaround, version cap, or partial fix instead of a full fix.
skill_candidates.evidence: 1. Open the tracking issue first, before finalising the PR body. 2. Reference it in the PR body by number ... 3. Add a link to the tracking issue as a comment at the workaround itself ... 5. The tracking issue should describe: what the workaround is, why it was chosen, the concrete follow-up work needed, and any acceptance criteria.

skill_candidates.summary: Populate the apache-magpie framework snapshot on a fresh clone before any framework skill (triage, code-review, stats, mentor) is usable.
skill_candidates.evidence: A fresh clone needs the snapshot populated before any framework skill is invocable. Run `/magpie-setup` (or follow `.claude/skills/magpie-setup/`) to fetch it per the committed `.apache-magpie.lock`.

always_on: Commands reference table for running tests/checks (pytest, breeze, ruff, mypy, docs build, selective-checks) — a usage catalog, not an obligation.
SQLite is the default backend; use --backend postgres/mysql for integration tests needing those databases; run docker network prune if Docker networking fails.
Repository Structure: UV workspace monorepo layout and key paths (airflow-core, task-sdk, providers, airflow-ctl, chart, dev, scripts) — informational.

Architecture Boundaries: component responsibilities and data-flow (Task SDK, Dag File Processor, Scheduler, Workers, API Server, Triggerer, shared libs, uv workspace) — design facts, not a single-action rule.

Security Model: distinguish actual vulnerabilities vs. known limitations vs. deployment hardening opportunities when reviewing code, writing security docs, or doing security research — a judgment framework, not tool-bindable.
Shared libraries structure and test-location facts (shared/<dist> workspace members, symlinked sources, where internal vs. consumer tests live).

Testing Standards general philosophy: target exactly 100% coverage of what the PR changes (not pre-existing logic), use conf_vars for config overrides, consolidate similar tests with @pytest.mark.parametrize, mark DB tests with @pytest.mark.db_test, test location mirrors source, fixtures live in devel-common/src/tests_common/pytest_plugin.py.
Output conventions: put generated files (PR reviews, reports, scratch output) under files/, creating the directory if it doesn't exist.
Prefer the magpie-pr-management-code-review skill over an ad-hoc review pass for PR code review (inline file:line comments, individual accept/skip flow, inline:off opt-out).
References section: links to contributing-docs (quick start, pull requests, local virtualenv, static checks, provider distributions, execution API versioning).
gh pr create --web body template mechanics (push branch, pre-filled body with Generated-by/AI-disclosure checkbox) — how-to detail, the actionable title constraint is already captured as a rule.
